title: aulomyrcia selloi
intro: Aulomyrcia selloi, also known as the Brazilian guava tree, is a species of flowering plant in the myrtle family, Myrtaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Aulomyrcia selloi include its ability to aid in digestion, reduce fever, and soothe respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures that can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
